page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.48xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by custom AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors. It features 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs operating at 2.7 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The memory configuration provides 768.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 4.0 GB per core. This instance runs on the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and includes a high-speed 200 Gbps baseline network interface.

Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 311,843.21 and passmark database operations reaching 47,160.91. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally strong, measuring 960.61 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 483.46 GB/sec for write operations. Single-core performance is moderate, as evidenced by average stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores. The server excels in parallelized tasks, achieving top-tier results in software compilation, ray tracing, and Redis SET operations, which reach 359.39 million operations per second.

The instance offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized workloads due to its dedicated ARM64 core architecture. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, and single-threaded tasks do not perform at the same top-tier level as multi-threaded operations. This server is highly suited for large-scale database operations, static web serving, multi-threaded software compilation, and containerized microservices.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.48xlarge is a general-purpose server utilizing the ARM64 architecture with 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs running at 2.7 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 768.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and a 200 Gbps baseline network speed, though it lacks local storage. Multi-core benchmarks place this server in the top tier, with exceptional memory bandwidth and high throughput for database operations and Redis SET commands. Single-core performance remains average, highlighting a tradeoff where parallelized workloads benefit most. This server is highly suited for large-scale web serving, database management, and multi-threaded software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.48xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 server featuring 192 dedicated cores, 768 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.48xlarge is an ARM64 server with 192 dedicated cores, 768 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps networking for high-density parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-density ARM64 platform delivering exceptional multi-core performance and massive memory bandwidth for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 192 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 cores at 2.7 GHz
Equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM and a fast 200 Gbps baseline network interface
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results
Optimized for database operations, Redis, and static web serving
Lacks local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions
